document.summary: The CSIS Global Health Policy Center is hosting an event on March 17 to discuss how private bio-pharmaceutical firms can enhance societal goals such as affordability, access, quality, and innovation. Sir Andrew Witty, CEO of GlaxoSmithKline, will deliver a keynote address on the company's reforms and their impact on global markets. The event will also feature a roundtable discussion with notable panelists, including Dr. Margaret Hamburg and Dr. Ezekiel Emmanuel, to delve deeper into these critical themes.
key_takeaways: Event on March 17 focuses on bio-pharmaceutical firms' societal roles.
Sir Andrew Witty will discuss GlaxoSmithKline's reforms.
Key themes include affordability, access, quality, and innovation.
Panelists include experts from the Institute of Medicine and University of Pennsylvania.
The event aims to strengthen trust in the bio-pharmaceutical industry.
Discussion will address transparency in clinical trials and pricing.
document.why_it_matters: This event is significant as it addresses the pressing need for the bio-pharmaceutical industry to align its practices with societal health goals, thereby enhancing public trust and improving health outcomes.

claims.claim_text: Private bio-pharmaceutical firms can better support societal goals.
claims.evidence_text: how private bio-pharmaceutical firms can better support the critically important societal goals of improved affordability, access, quality, and innovation.

events.evidence_text: Please join the CSIS Global Health Policy Center on March 17, from 10:00am – 11:30am, for an exploration of how private bio-pharmaceutical firms can better support the critically important societal goals.
